title: Asha's Friendship Garden
synopsis: When Asha's family moves to a new neighborhood, she feels lonely and misses her old friends. But when she discovers an empty garden plot in her backyard, she has an idea that brings together children from all around the neighborhood to create something beautiful together.

pages.text: Asha looked out the window of her new house. Everything was different here. The trees were taller. The houses were different colors. Even the mailboxes looked strange. "I miss my old house," Asha said with a big sigh. "I miss my friends Maya and Sam."

illustration.description: A young South Asian girl with long dark braids and big expressive brown eyes looks sadly out a large window. Her face shows longing and melancholy as she presses her hand against the glass. The view outside shows a sunny suburban street with colorful houses, tall oak trees, and unique mailboxes.
illustration.alt: Asha looking sadly out her new house window at the unfamiliar neighborhood

pages.text: Mom came over and gave Asha a warm hug. "I know it's hard to leave our old home," Mom said. "But this new place could be wonderful too. We just need to give it a chance." Dad peeked his head around the corner. "Want to explore the backyard with me?" he asked with a smile.

illustration.description: A loving family scene with Asha's mother, a warm woman with kind eyes and a colorful headscarf, hugging Asha gently. Dad, a tall man with a friendly beard and bright smile, peeks around the doorframe with an encouraging expression. The room has moving boxes and warm afternoon sunlight streaming in.
illustration.alt: Asha's parents comforting her, with moving boxes in the background

pages.text: In the backyard, Asha found something interesting. There was a big empty space with good dirt and lots of sunshine. "What's this space for?" Asha asked. "The lady who lived here before had a garden," Dad explained. "Now it's ours to do whatever we want with it." Asha's eyes got bigger. She was starting to get an idea.

illustration.description: Asha and her dad stand in a sunny backyard looking at a large rectangular plot of rich, dark soil surrounded by a small wooden border. The garden space is empty but full of potential, with dappled sunlight filtering through leafy trees above. Asha's expression shows growing curiosity and excitement.
illustration.alt: Asha and Dad discovering the empty garden plot in their new backyard

pages.text: That night, Asha had a wonderful dream about flowers and vegetables growing everywhere. When she woke up, she ran to find Mom and Dad. "I want to plant a garden!" she said excitedly. "But not just any garden. A friendship garden!" "What's a friendship garden?" Mom asked with interest.

illustration.description: Asha sits up in bed with messy braids and pajamas covered in flower patterns, her eyes sparkling with excitement and her arms stretched wide. Dream-like flowers and vegetables float around her head in soft, magical colors. Her bedroom has unpacked boxes and a cozy, lived-in feeling with morning sunlight.
illustration.alt: Asha waking up excitedly with her garden idea, surrounded by dreamy floating plants

pages.text: "We can invite kids from the neighborhood to help plant it," Asha explained. "Everyone can choose something different to grow. Then we can all be friends!" Dad clapped his hands together. "That's a fantastic idea!" he said. "Let's make some invitations right after breakfast."

illustration.description: Asha sits at a sunny kitchen table with her parents, gesturing enthusiastically as she explains her idea. Her parents look delighted and impressed. The kitchen has warm yellow walls, cheerful dishes, and a welcoming atmosphere. Asha's expression is animated and full of hope.
illustration.alt: Asha excitedly explaining her friendship garden idea to her parents at breakfast

pages.text: Asha spent all morning making colorful invitations. She drew pictures of flowers and wrote in her best letters: "Come plant with me! Friendship Garden Party! Saturday at 10!" She made ten invitations with different colored flowers on each one. "Now I just have to be brave enough to give them out," she whispered.

illustration.description: Asha sits cross-legged on the living room floor surrounded by art supplies - crayons, markers, colored paper, and glue sticks. She's carefully drawing flowers on bright invitations, her tongue sticking out slightly in concentration. Finished invitations with different colored flowers are spread around her like a rainbow.
illustration.alt: Asha making colorful garden party invitations on the floor with art supplies

pages.text: Asha took a deep breath and walked to the first house. A boy about her age was playing with toy cars in the driveway. "Hi," Asha said quietly. "I'm Asha. I just moved here." The boy looked up and smiled. "I'm Marcus! I saw the moving truck. Cool! Are you going to my school?"

illustration.description: Asha stands nervously at the edge of a driveway holding a bright invitation, approaching a cheerful Black boy with short curly hair who's kneeling beside several toy cars. Marcus looks up with a genuine, welcoming smile. The setting shows a friendly suburban home with a basketball hoop and colorful chalk art on the driveway.
illustration.alt: Asha nervously approaching Marcus who is playing with toy cars in his driveway

pages.text: "Yes! I start Monday," Asha said, feeling a little braver. She handed Marcus the invitation. "Would you like to come to my garden party?" Marcus read the invitation and grinned. "This sounds awesome! I love digging in dirt. Can I bring my little sister Luna? She loves flowers." "Of course!" Asha said happily.

illustration.description: Marcus holds the colorful invitation with excitement, his face lit up with joy. Asha's posture shows she's becoming more confident, standing straighter with a growing smile. Behind them, a small girl with pigtails peeks curiously from behind the front door of their house. The scene radiates warmth and growing friendship.
illustration.alt: Marcus excitedly reading the invitation while his little sister peeks from the doorway

pages.text: Asha visited more houses on her street. She met Sofia, who spoke Spanish and English and loved tomatoes. She met twins Jake and James who wanted to grow pumpkins for Halloween. She met Mrs. Chen, who offered to bring flower seeds from her own garden. By the end of the day, Asha had given out all her invitations!

illustration.description: A montage-style illustration showing Asha meeting different neighbors: Sofia, a girl with long dark hair and a bright dress; elderly Mrs. Chen with silver hair and kind eyes holding seed packets; and twin boys with freckles and matching striped shirts. Each neighbor holds a colorful invitation, and the background shows the diverse, welcoming neighborhood.
illustration.alt: Asha meeting various diverse neighbors, each holding one of her colorful invitations

pages.text: Saturday morning came with perfect sunshine. Asha woke up early and helped Dad set up tables in the backyard. Mom made lemonade and cookies. At exactly 10 o'clock, the doorbell rang. "They came!" Asha shouted with joy. She ran to open the door and found all her new neighbors waiting with big smiles.

illustration.description: Asha's backyard is set up for a party with folding tables covered in cheerful tablecloths, pitchers of lemonade, and plates of cookies. Asha runs toward the back door with pure excitement, her braids flying behind her. The garden plot is ready with small tools and watering cans arranged nearby. Golden morning sunlight bathes everything.
illustration.alt: Asha's backyard set up for the garden party with tables, refreshments, and gardening supplies

pages.text: Everyone brought something special to plant. Marcus and Luna brought sunflower seeds. Sofia brought tiny tomato plants. The twins brought pumpkin seeds. Mrs. Chen brought marigold seeds that sparkled like tiny jewels. "This is going to be the most beautiful garden ever!" Asha declared as everyone gathered around the empty plot.

illustration.description: All the neighborhood children and Mrs. Chen stand in a happy circle around the garden plot, each holding different seeds or small plants. Their faces beam with excitement and anticipation. The diverse group includes Marcus with sunflower seeds, Sofia with tomato plants, the twins with pumpkin seeds, and Mrs. Chen with sparkling marigold seeds.
illustration.alt: All the neighbors gathered around the garden plot, each holding their seeds or plants to contribute

pages.text: Everyone worked together, digging holes and planting seeds. They laughed when Luna got dirt on her nose. They cheered when Marcus found a friendly worm. Sofia taught everyone the Spanish word for garden: "jardín." When they finished, they all stood back to admire their work. "Now we just wait for it to grow," said Mrs. Chen wisely.

illustration.description: The children are actively gardening together, kneeling in the dirt with small shovels and watering cans. Luna has a cute smudge of dirt on her nose and is giggling. Marcus holds up a worm proudly. The garden plot now shows organized rows of planted areas. Everyone looks happy and dirty from their hard work.
illustration.alt: All the children working together in the garden, laughing and getting dirty while planting

pages.text: Weeks later, the friendship garden was full of growing plants. But more importantly, Asha was full of new friends. She played with Marcus and Luna every day. She learned to make salsa with Sofia's tomatoes. She couldn't wait for the twins' pumpkins to grow. "Mom was right," Asha thought happily. "This new place is wonderful after all."

illustration.description: The garden is now lush and thriving with tall sunflowers, green tomato plants, spreading pumpkin vines, and bright marigolds. Asha stands in the center with all her new friends, their arms around each other in a group hug. Everyone is smiling radiantly. The garden represents their flourishing friendship, vibrant and full of life.
illustration.alt: Asha and all her new friends hugging in their beautiful, thriving friendship garden

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: How did Asha feel when she first moved to her new house?
questions.explanation: Asha felt sad and lonely because she missed her old house and her friends Maya and Sam.

questions.question: What did Asha discover in her backyard?
questions.explanation: Asha found an empty garden space with good dirt and sunshine that used to belong to the previous owner.

questions.question: What was special about Asha's garden idea?
choices: It would be a friendship garden where neighbors could plant together
It would be the biggest garden ever
questions.explanation: Asha wanted to create a friendship garden where she could invite neighborhood kids to plant together and make new friends.

questions.question: Who was the first person Asha met when delivering invitations?
questions.explanation: Marcus was the first person Asha met. He was playing with toy cars in his driveway when she approached him.

questions.question: What happened to the friendship garden at the end of the story?
choices: It died because no one watered it
It grew beautifully and Asha made many new friends
It was too small for all the plants
questions.explanation: The friendship garden grew beautifully with sunflowers, tomatoes, pumpkins, and marigolds, and Asha made wonderful new friendships with all her neighbors.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Asha's New Friends
pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence about Asha's story.

sentences.text: Asha felt ___ when she moved to her new house.
She found an empty ___ space in her backyard.
Asha made colorful ___ to give to her neighbors.

pages.title: Complete the Garden Party
pages.instructions: Help finish the sentences about what everyone brought to plant.

sentences.text: Marcus and Luna brought ___ seeds to plant.
Sofia brought tiny ___ plants from her home.
Mrs. Chen brought sparkly ___ seeds to share.
